name: Reginald D. Hunter
bio: Reginald D. Hunter is an American stand-up comedian based in the United Kingdom, born in Albany, Georgia, who moved to London in 1996 and began comedy as a dare. Known for a measured, straight-talking delivery and material on race, identity and culture, he was nominated for the Perrier Award three years running in the early 2000s. He has fronted BBC documentary series on American music.

timeline.ev: Moves to London and begins stand-up
Appears on Channel 4s The 11 OClock Show
Perrier Best Newcomer nomination for debut full show
Wins the Writers Guild Award for Comedy
Fronts BBC Two series Songs of the South
